name: Quantizing Recursive Reasoning Models

description: arXiv:2607.16237v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Recursive reasoning models solve hard puzzles by applying compact, weight-tied blocks over many refinement steps. Because these blocks are reused many times, quantizing them creates a unique dynamical problem: the quantization error is incurred at every step. While 8-bit quantization (integer or float) preserves accuracy, moving to a per-tensor...
author: Thorir Mar Ingolfsson, Wajeeha Tahir, Anna Tegon, Lionnus Kesting, Gamze \.Islamo\u{g}lu, Luca Benini
